message:
feat: GitHub Action — composite action for PR PII scanning

Composite action at repo root (action.yml) that wraps the same
precommit.js scan script as the local hook, but adds:

- actions/cache integration to persist the 770 MB model across
  runs (cache key = textsift version + runner OS + arch)
- npx-based pre-warm step (no-op on cache hit)
- File selection modes: 'changed' (default — PR diff or last
  commit on push), 'all' (every tracked file), or a custom glob
- GitHub workflow command annotations (::error / ::warning)
  emitted by precommit.js when GITHUB_ACTIONS=true is set, so
  findings appear inline on the PR's "Files changed" tab

Inputs: files, severity, warn-only, secrets, textsift-version,
cache-dir. All optional; the minimal usage is just `uses:
teamchong/textsift@v1`.

Annotation format follows GitHub's spec: percent and newlines
in preview text are escaped so the workflow command parser
doesn't misinterpret them. Verified by 3 new precommit tests
(GitHub::error emission, --warn-only switches to ::warning,
escape-correctness).

Docs page (/textsift/github-action/) covers install, all inputs,
common workflow patterns (block / soft-warn / required-status),
and a comparison table vs gitleaks-action / trufflehog /
super-linter that explains where textsift's coverage doesn't
overlap (names, emails, addresses, phones in arbitrary text).

Sidebar updated.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,125 @@
+name: 'textsift PII scan'
+description: 'Scan files for personally identifiable info using openai/privacy-filter. Runs entirely on the GitHub runner; no data leaves CI.'
+author: 'teamchong'
+branding:
+  icon: 'shield'
+  color: 'purple'
+
+inputs:
+  files:
+    description: |
+      Files to scan. Either a glob (e.g. `'src/**/*.{ts,md}'`) or `'changed'`
+      (default in PR context — scans only the diff). Set to `'all'` to scan
+      every tracked file in the repo.
+    required: false
+    default: 'changed'
+  severity:
+    description: |
+      `'block'` (default) blocks on all model PII + severity:block rule
+      spans. `'warn'` only blocks on rule:block spans (model spans become
+      warnings). `'all'` blocks on anything detected.
+    required: false
+    default: 'block'
+  warn-only:
+    description: |
+      `'true'` to emit findings as workflow warnings without failing the
+      check. Default: `'false'` — the action fails the check on blocking
+      findings.
+    required: false
+    default: 'false'
+  secrets:
+    description: |
+      `'true'` (default) enables the built-in secrets rule preset
+      (JWT, GitHub PAT, AWS, Slack, OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Stripe keys,
+      PEM private keys). Set to `'false'` to disable if you already use
+      gitleaks or trufflehog for secrets.
+    required: false
+    default: 'true'
+  textsift-version:
+    description: |
+      npm version of textsift to use. Default: `'latest'`. Pin to a tag
+      (e.g. `'0.1.0'`) for reproducible CI.
+    required: false
+    default: 'latest'
+  cache-dir:
+    description: |
+      Override the model cache directory. Default: runner's `~/.cache/textsift`.
+      Pair with `actions/cache` to persist the 770 MB model across runs.
+    required: false
+    default: ''
+
+runs:
+  using: 'composite'
+  steps:
+    - name: Restore textsift model cache
+      uses: actions/cache@v4
+      with:
+        path: ${{ inputs.cache-dir != '' && inputs.cache-dir || '~/.cache/textsift' }}
+        key: textsift-model-${{ inputs.textsift-version }}-${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}
+        restore-keys: |
+          textsift-model-${{ inputs.textsift-version }}-${{ runner.os }}-
+
+    - name: Pre-warm model cache
+      shell: bash
+      env:
+        TEXTSIFT_CACHE_DIR: ${{ inputs.cache-dir }}
+      run: |
+        # `--no-prompt` prevents the TTY confirm; `download` is a no-op
+        # if the cache is already warm from actions/cache restore.
+        npx -y textsift@${{ inputs.textsift-version }} download --no-prompt
+
+    - name: Determine files to scan
+      id: files
+      shell: bash
+      run: |
+        case "${{ inputs.files }}" in
+          changed)
+            if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
+              git fetch origin ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }} --depth=1 2>/dev/null || true
+              files=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT \
+                origin/${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}...HEAD)
+            else
+              # Push event — diff against the previous commit.
+              files=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMRT HEAD~1 HEAD 2>/dev/null || git ls-files)
+            fi
+            ;;
+          all)
+            files=$(git ls-files)
+            ;;
+          *)
+            # Treat as a glob pattern.
+            files=$(git ls-files "${{ inputs.files }}")
+            ;;
+        esac
+        # Write file list to a temp file (avoid argv length limits + quoting).
+        echo "$files" > /tmp/textsift-files.txt
+        echo "count=$(wc -l < /tmp/textsift-files.txt | tr -d ' ')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+        echo "Files to consider: $(wc -l < /tmp/textsift-files.txt | tr -d ' ')"
+
+    - name: Scan for PII
+      shell: bash
+      env:
+        TEXTSIFT_OFFLINE: '1'
+        TEXTSIFT_CACHE_DIR: ${{ inputs.cache-dir }}
+        TEXTSIFT_PRECOMMIT_SEVERITY: ${{ inputs.severity }}
+        TEXTSIFT_PRECOMMIT_SECRETS: ${{ inputs.secrets == 'true' && '1' || '0' }}
+      run: |
+        if [ "${{ steps.files.outputs.count }}" = "0" ]; then
+          echo "No files to scan."
+          exit 0
+        fi
+        # Resolve the precommit.js script from the installed package.
+        # `npm root -g` gives the global modules dir where `npx -y` cached
+        # textsift during the pre-warm step.
+        SCRIPT="$(npm root -g)/textsift/scripts/precommit.js"
+        if [ ! -f "$SCRIPT" ]; then
+          # Fallback: fetch and run via `npx` directly.
+          xargs -a /tmp/textsift-files.txt npx -y textsift@${{ inputs.textsift-version }} \
+            --no-prompt detect --jsonl
+          exit $?
+        fi
+        # Pass file list as args. The script emits ::error / ::warning
+        # workflow commands itself when GITHUB_ACTIONS=true.
+        EXTRA=()
+        if [ "${{ inputs.warn-only }}" = "true" ]; then EXTRA+=("--warn-only"); fi
+        xargs -a /tmp/textsift-files.txt node "$SCRIPT" "${EXTRA[@]}"

@@ -37,6 +37,7 @@ export default defineConfig({
         { label: "Quickstart", slug: "quickstart" },
         { label: "CLI", slug: "cli" },
         { label: "Pre-commit hook", slug: "precommit" },
+        { label: "GitHub Action", slug: "github-action" },
         { label: "Playground", slug: "playground" },
         { label: "Faker mode playground", slug: "playground-faker" },
         { label: "API Reference", slug: "api" },

@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+---
+title: GitHub Action
+description: Block PRs that introduce PII. Composite Action that wraps the textsift CLI; runs entirely on the GitHub runner.
+---
+
+textsift ships a [composite GitHub Action](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/creating-actions/creating-a-composite-action) at the repo root. Add it to a workflow and PRs that introduce PII fail the check, with inline annotations on the offending lines.
+
+Same engine as the [CLI](/textsift/cli/) and the [pre-commit hook](/textsift/precommit/). Runs entirely on the GitHub runner; **no source code or detected PII leaves CI**.
+
+## Quick start
+
+```yaml
+# .github/workflows/pii.yml
+name: PII scan
+on: [pull_request, push]
+
+jobs:
+  scan:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0   # need full history for the diff
+      - uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+```
+
+That's the minimum. The action handles cache, model download, file selection (PR diff vs full-tree), scanning, and inline PR annotations.
+
+## Inputs
+
+| Input | Default | Effect |
+|---|---|---|
+| `files` | `'changed'` | `changed` — only PR diff (or last-commit diff on push). `all` — every tracked file. Glob like `'src/**/*.{ts,md}'` for an explicit set. |
+| `severity` | `'block'` | `block` (model PII + rule:block spans), `warn` (only rule:block), `all` (anything detected). |
+| `warn-only` | `'false'` | `'true'` to emit warnings without failing the check. |
+| `secrets` | `'true'` | Set to `'false'` if you already use gitleaks / trufflehog. |
+| `textsift-version` | `'latest'` | npm version. Pin to a tag (`'0.1.0'`) for reproducible CI. |
+| `cache-dir` | runner default | Override the model cache location. |
+
+## Common patterns
+
+### Strict PR check (block bad PRs)
+
+```yaml
+- uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+  with:
+    severity: block
+    files: changed
+```
+
+### Soft warning (annotate but don't fail)
+
+```yaml
+- uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+  with:
+    warn-only: 'true'
+```
+
+Useful for legacy codebases — adds annotations to surface findings in PR review without blocking ship.
+
+### Scan a specific path
+
+```yaml
+- uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+  with:
+    files: 'docs/**/*.md'
+```
+
+Glob is passed to `git ls-files`; respects `.gitignore`.
+
+### Skip secrets scanning (delegated to gitleaks)
+
+```yaml
+- uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
+- uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+  with:
+    secrets: 'false'
+```
+
+textsift's regex secrets preset overlaps with gitleaks. If gitleaks is your secrets baseline, let textsift focus on what only the model catches (names, emails, phones, addresses, etc.).
+
+### Pin the version for reproducible CI
+
+```yaml
+- uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+  with:
+    textsift-version: '0.1.0'
+```
+
+Otherwise `latest` is pulled at each run; new minor versions can change the model output.
+
+## How it works under the hood
+
+The composite action runs four steps:
+
+1. **Restore cache** — `actions/cache` restores `~/.cache/textsift/` from the previous run if available. Cache key includes textsift version + runner OS + arch so unrelated upgrades don't churn it.
+2. **Pre-warm model** — `npx textsift download --no-prompt`. No-op on cache hit; downloads the ~770 MB model on miss.
+3. **Determine files** — uses `git diff` for PR / push, or `git ls-files` for `all`. File list goes to a tempfile (avoids argv length limits).
+4. **Scan** — invokes `precommit.js` (the same hook that runs locally) which loads the model once, scans all files in-process, and emits GitHub `::error` / `::warning` workflow commands so findings show up as PR annotations on the right lines.
+
+## Performance
+
+| Scenario | Cold (first PR) | Warm (cache hit) |
+|---|---|---|
+| Linux runner, 50-file PR | ~3 min (model download) | ~15 sec |
+| Linux runner, 1000-file `all` scan | ~5 min | ~2 min |
+
+The cold-start cost is dominated by the 770 MB download. Once the cache is populated, subsequent runs across PRs hit `actions/cache` and start in seconds.
+
+## Annotations
+
+When findings exist, the action emits GitHub workflow commands so each finding shows up as an inline annotation on the PR's "Files changed" tab:
+
+```
+::error file=src/test.ts,line=42,col=18,title=textsift PII (private_person)::Found "Alice Carter"
+```
+
+`warn-only: true` switches `error` to `warning` so the check still passes but reviewers see the spans.
+
+## CI workflow examples
+
+### Just-block-the-PR (most common)
+
+```yaml
+name: PII scan
+on:
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main]
+
+jobs:
+  pii:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
+      - uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+```
+
+### Required-status-check + soft warn
+
+Make a separate "required" job for severity:`block` and a "warn" job that's allowed to fail:
+
+```yaml
+jobs:
+  pii-block:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
+      - uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+        with:
+          severity: block
+          secrets: 'true'
+
+  pii-suggest:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    continue-on-error: true
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
+      - uses: teamchong/textsift@v1
+        with:
+          severity: all
+          warn-only: 'true'
+```
+
+The first job makes the PR red on real findings; the second annotates *every* detected span as a suggestion without blocking.
+
+## Comparison vs other actions
+
+| Action | What | Where it overlaps |
+|---|---|---|
+| [gitleaks-action](https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks-action) | Regex secrets | Heavy overlap with textsift's `secrets` preset — disable `secrets` in textsift if you use gitleaks. |
+| [trufflehog-actions-scan](https://github.com/trufflesecurity/trufflehog) | Verified secrets (validates against APIs) | Doesn't overlap — trufflehog confirms a secret is real; textsift catches everything that *looks* like one. Use both for layered defence. |
+| [super-linter](https://github.com/super-linter/super-linter) | Polyglot linting (eslint, rubocop, etc.) | No PII detection. Run alongside textsift. |
+
+textsift catches what regex-only tools can't: **names, emails, addresses, phone numbers, dates, account numbers in arbitrary text**. PRs that copy a real customer name into a code comment, or a personal phone number into a README, get caught here and not by the secrets scanners.
